skills.name: Asana
skills.description: Guide for managing tasks and projects in Asana. Use when the user asks to create tasks, search Asana, check project status, move tasks between sections, or comment on Asana tasks.
skills.changelog: Initial release

skills.name: Brand Naming
skills.description: Research-backed brand naming co-pilot for companies, products, and features. Generates candidates through five creative lenses (archetype, phonetic, competitive gap, metaphor, wild card), evaluates against trademark distinctiveness and domain strategy, and produces a shortlist with rationale and tagline drafts. Use when naming a product, company, feature, or project — or when the user asks "what should we call this."
skills.changelog: Major rewrite with 5 creative lenses, Neumeier evaluation rubric, and domain strategy

skills.name: Code Review
skills.description: Use when reviewing code, pull requests, or diffs. Covers security, performance, readability, and maintainability checks with structured feedback.
skills.changelog: Initial release

skills.name: PM E2E Testing
skills.description: Run E2E browser tests against any live or dev web app from a plain English journey description. No codebase required. Use when testing user flows, verifying features, or running ad-hoc journey tests on any web URL.
skills.changelog: Initial release

skills.name: Meeting Notes
skills.description: Use when summarizing meetings, extracting action items, writing follow-ups, or organizing discussion points. Produces structured notes from raw conversation.
skills.changelog: Initial release

skills.name: Design: Luxury
skills.description: Modern, bold visual style with big headings, a dark surface, white text, and Oswald typeface. Use when building UI in a luxury feel or when the user asks for the luxury design system.
skills.changelog: Added design tokens, color palette, and component specs

skills.name: Design: Refined
skills.description: Modern, minimal visual style with a clean white surface, Playfair Display typeface, and blue/purple accents. Use when building UI in a refined feel or when the user asks for the refined design system.
skills.changelog: Added design tokens, color palette, and component specs

skills.name: Design: Dramatic
skills.description: High-contrast, theatrical visual style with a near-black surface, purple and rose accents, and Outfit typeface. Use when building UI in a dramatic, immersive feel or when the user asks for the dramatic design system.
skills.changelog: Added design tokens, color palette, and component specs

skills.name: Design: Bold
skills.description: Heavy, high-impact visual style with a dark surface, blue and green accents, and Archivo Black typeface. Use when building UI in a bold, attention-grabbing feel or when the user asks for the bold design system.
skills.changelog: Added design tokens, color palette, and component specs

skills.name: Design: Bento
skills.description: Grid-based visual style with content in bento-box blocks of varying sizes, warm peach and beige tones, and Inter typeface. Use when building bento grid layouts or UI with a warm, modular feel.
skills.changelog: Added design tokens, color palette, and component specs

skills.name: Design: Neobrutalism
skills.description: Raw, high-contrast visual style with bold borders, yellow and deep blue accents on a light surface, and Inter typeface. Use when building neobrutalist UI or when the user asks for the neobrutalism design system.
skills.changelog: Added design tokens, color palette, and component specs

skills.name: Business Strategy
skills.description: Research-backed strategic co-pilot for a startup idea. Runs parallel research across market, competition, customer, business model, and GTM, then writes an opinionated memo with explicit bets and open questions. Use when evaluating a new idea, validating a market, building a strategy from scratch, or when the user shares an idea and asks "what do you think."
skills.changelog: Substantial expansion of research-and-synthesis workflow; sharper opinionated memo template with explicit bets.

skills.name: Persona Builder
skills.description: Build synthetic personas for business strategy using the JTBD-native framework. Guides segment mapping, community grounding, and collaborative template construction. Produces Sketch, Grounded, or Full tier persona artifacts for use in positioning, messaging, and GTM.
skills.changelog: Refinements to JTBD-native template and community-grounding step.

skills.name: Pricing Strategy
skills.description: Research-backed pricing co-pilot for early-stage hardware and software products. Runs parallel research across competitive price maps, willingness-to-pay signals, cost benchmarks, and demand-side evidence, then writes a pricing plan with a recommended price, tier structure, and customer-facing narrative. Use when figuring out what to charge, picking a pricing model, or answering "what's the right price."
skills.changelog: Expanded WTP/COGS/demand research stages; richer SKU architecture and pricing-narrative output.

skills.name: GTM Strategy
skills.description: Research-backed go-to-market co-pilot for early-stage startups. Runs parallel research across beachhead selection, competitive GTM, first-customer patterns, and channel intelligence, then writes an opinionated GTM plan with a specific motion, channel bets, and a sequenced first-100-customers plan. Use when figuring out how to get first customers, picking a sales motion, or building a go-to-market from scratch.
skills.changelog: Expanded beachhead/competitive-GTM/first-customer/channel research; tighter first-100-customers sequencing.

skills.name: GTM Simulator
skills.description: GTM and business simulation engine. Takes a GTM plan, personas, and brand brief, then runs a time-horizon simulation that makes plan actions concrete, models realistic market responses, and surfaces gaps and broken assumptions. Delivers a findings report with metric scorecard, assumption tracker, and recommendations. Use when stress-testing a plan, simulating year 1, or asking "what would actually happen if."
skills.changelog: Tightened branch-comparison and findings-report structure; clearer assumption tracker.

skills.name: Competitive Landscape
skills.description: Research-backed competitive landscape analysis for new markets, products, and ventures. Runs parallel research across market players, positioning, product intelligence, momentum signals, and customer voice, then writes a brief with positioning maps, threat scorecards, open-space analysis, and moat assessment. Use when mapping competition for a new venture, refreshing an existing analysis, or asking "who else is doing this."
skills.changelog: Major expansion: deeper parallel research (players, positioning, momentum signals, customer voice), richer threat scorecards and moat assessment, expanded synthesis stage.

skills.name: Threat Analysis
skills.description: Structured, calibrated threat assessment for personal and family safety. Produces a ThreatIndex score (0-10) with 5-dimensional breakdown, calibrated probability language, and tiered action recommendations. Draws on professional intelligence methodology.
skills.changelog: Substantial expansion: more rigorous 5-dimension scoring, more calibrated probability language, expanded tiered action recommendations.

skills.name: Research
skills.description: Guide systematic research workflows — check existing knowledge before external searches, state confidence levels, cite sources, and save findings as bits for future reference. Use when researching, investigating, comparing options, or evaluating alternatives.
skills.changelog: Initial release

skills.name: Pitch
skills.description: Narrative synthesis co-pilot that turns strategy artifacts into stories that make people excited. Pulls in whatever upstream strategy outputs exist (memo, personas, GTM plan, brand brief, pricing) and produces three things: a scrollytelling narrative, a shareable one-pager, and tiered talking points (30s / 3min / 10min). Use when writing a pitch, an elevator pitch, a one-pager, or figuring out how to tell the story.
skills.changelog: Expanded Raskin + Duarte synthesis stages; richer one-pager and tiered talking-points output.

skills.name: Brand Brief
skills.description: Collaborative brand identity construction through a structured 13-section brief. Covers Onliness Statement, voice specification, visual direction, naming territory, and an AI-readable layer. Bridges strategy and execution — receives positioning from business-strategy and feeds brand-naming and visual work.
skills.changelog: Refined 13-section brief structure with tighter Onliness Statement and voice specification.

skills.name: Strategic Planning
skills.description: Guide strategic thinking sessions before committing to work items. Three-stage process: Frame (define the question), Explore (research and options), Decide (commit with reasoning). Use when facing a decision that deserves structured thinking before action.
skills.changelog: Initial release

skills.name: Accessibility Review
skills.description: Accessibility quality review for web applications. Covers semantic HTML, ARIA patterns, keyboard interaction, focus management, and automated testing with axe-core. Use when reviewing UI for WCAG compliance or building accessible components.
skills.changelog: Initial release

skills.name: Design Inspiration
skills.description: Find real-world design inspiration for websites, apps, and UI patterns. Searches and curates 5-8 examples with "steal this" notes and cross-cutting pattern analysis, then generates a dark-themed gallery page with desktop + mobile screenshots. Use when looking for design references for a pattern you're building — pricing pages, dashboards, event cards, landing pages, navigation, portfolios.
skills.changelog: Refined gallery generation and pattern-analysis output.

skills.name: HTML Prototype
skills.description: Load before building HTML prototypes, mockups, wireframes, or UI explorations. Contains generation rules that prevent common AI quality issues like generic layouts and slop patterns.
skills.changelog: Initial release

skills.name: Publish Skill
skills.description: Publish one or more skills to the public Tink Library catalog on GitHub. The catalog is consumed by Tink Tools, TinkerTerm, and T2 (TinkChat) — once pushed, the skill appears in every consumer. Use when the user wants to add a skill to the catalog, publish a skill, or "push this to Tink Library."
skills.changelog: Initial entry — proof of folder-shaped catalog support (Epic 286 Story 004)

skills.name: UI Build (Figma → Code)
skills.description: Figma → HTML/CSS/React implementation workflow. Use when translating a Figma design into working code. Enforces the right order: read the design spec first, then map to design tokens, then build. Pairs with ui-verify.
skills.changelog: Initial release — Figma-to-code build workflow.

skills.name: UI Verify (Figma → Code)
skills.description: Visual verification for Figma → HTML/CSS/React work. Use after any visual change. Enforces checking code against the Figma spec AND screenshots against the design, across both viewports, before declaring done. Pairs with ui-build.
skills.changelog: Initial release — Figma-to-code visual verification workflow.
